historyTimeline.h2: Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged Engine History & Production Timeline
historyTimeline.intro: The Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged launched in 2012, powered exclusively by the Jaguar Land Rover AJ133 5.0-litre V8 petrol engine family. This high-performance powertrain debuted with 510 PS (503 bhp) and 625 Nm of torque, establishing the LWB variant as the flagship luxury SUV for global markets. Throughout its production run, the engine architecture remained consistent, though output was increased to 550 PS (542 bhp) and later 565 PS (557 bhp) for the SVAutobiography Dynamic editions introduced in 2017. The variant remains in production today, continuing to offer the naturally aspirated smoothness and supercharged urgency characteristic of the AJ133 platform, despite the industry-wide shift towards electrified powertrains.
milestones: 2012 - AJ133P (510 PS) introduced with 625 Nm torque, marking the launch of the L405 generation and replacing the previous L322’s 5.0L supercharged unit with improved efficiency and power density.
2017 - AJ133P (550 PS) calibrated for SVAutobiography Dynamic, increasing power to 404 kW and torque to 680 Nm for enhanced performance in the flagship SV trim.
2019 - AJ133P (565 PS) uprated for SVAutobiography LWB and Dynamic models, delivering 557 bhp and maintaining 680 Nm torque to compete with emerging high-performance luxury SUVs.
Present - Still in production, with the 5.0L Supercharged V8 remaining the top-tier petrol option for the L405 LWB alongside diesel and mild-hybrid six-cylinder alternatives.
specs: Power Output: 510 bhp / 510 PS (Standard); 557 bhp / 565 PS (SVAutobiography Dynamic)
Torque: 625 Nm / 461 lb-ft (Standard); 680 Nm / 502 lb-ft (SVAutobiography Dynamic)

commonProblems.h2: Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged, powered by the AJ133 V8 engine family (codes RCBW/RCBV), delivers exceptional performance but faces specific high-mileage failure patterns documented across UK owner forums and specialist rebuilders. Every rebuilt Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded failure-point components and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this guide to compare repair options and get tailored quotes from certified UK specialists.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner & Guide Failure - Why Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged AJ133 Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged Autobiography, SVAutobiography, Vogue SE (2013-2022, AJ133/RCBW)
problems.rootCause: The stainless steel pin on the timing chain tensioner wears a groove into the aluminium chain guide, reducing tension and allowing chain slap; this is corroborated across multiple fullfatrr.com and PistonHeads UK forum threads discussing cold-start rattles and cam/crank synchronisation faults.

repairOptions.dealerPrice: £1,850-£2,450 (estimated based on standard UK dealer rates)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain tensioners with uprated hardened-disc units, inspect guides for wear
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative fix; 40,000-60,000 mile extension if caught before guide damage

repairOptions.tier: Major Repair - Full timing system overhaul
repairOptions.dealerPrice: £4,200-£5,800 (estimated based on standard UK dealer rates)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain kit, tensioners, guides, VVT sprockets, and front cover seals
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory timing; suitable if block/head undamaged

repairOptions.dealerPrice: £9,500-£12,500 (estimated based on standard UK dealer rates)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete stripped block rebuild with upgraded timing components, new bearings/seals, bench-tested, ECU-matched, 12-month warranty. Workshop-fitted.
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution when chain jump has caused piston/valve damage

problems.recommendation: If you hear a brief rattling noise on cold start but have no engine management lights, a specialist tensioner replacement may prevent catastrophic failure. However, once cam/crank codes appear or metallic rattling persists, the risk of piston damage is high - on a Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged valued at £25,000-£45,000, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components offers better long-term value than repeated major repairs.
problems.cta: -> Compare rebuilt AJ133 engine prices for your Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged

problems.h4: Spun Main or Conrod Bearings - Why Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged AJ133 Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged Autobiography, SVAutobiography (2014-2022, AJ133/RCBW)
problems.rootCause: Extended oil change intervals combined with high-load driving can lead to oil starvation at the bearings; UK forum reports on DiscoWeb and rangerovers.net describe sudden loss of oil pressure followed by knocking, with oil analysis confirming bearing material in sump.

repairOptions.tier: Minor Repair - Oil system flush + bearing inspection
repairOptions.dealerPrice: £850-£1,350 (estimated based on standard UK dealer rates)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain oil, inspect sump for bearing debris, pressure-test oil pump, replace oil filter and cooler lines
repairOptions.longevity: Diagnostic step only; not a repair if bearings are already damaged

repairOptions.dealerPrice: £6,200-£8,400 (estimated based on standard UK dealer rates)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, replace main/conrod bearings, regrind or replace crankshaft, new oil pump and pickup
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if cylinder walls and heads are undamaged; 30,000-50,000 mile expectation

repairOptions.dealerPrice: £9,500-£12,500 (estimated based on standard UK dealer rates)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete stripped block rebuild with upgraded bearing clearances, new pistons/rings if needed, bench-tested, ECU-matched, 12-month warranty. Workshop-fitted.
repairOptions.longevity: Only reliable solution once bearing material has circulated through the engine

problems.recommendation: If your Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged shows low oil pressure warnings or metallic knocking, stop driving immediately - continued operation will destroy the crankshaft. For vehicles with over 80,000 miles, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded bearings and oiling components is more cost-effective than a bottom-end rebuild that leaves aged top-end components at risk.
problems.cta: -> Compare rebuilt AJ133 engine prices for your Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged

problems.h4: Supercharger Coupling & Bearing Wear - Why Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged AJ133 Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged Vogue, Autobiography, SVAutobiography (2013-2022, AJ133/RCBW)
problems.rootCause: The supercharger isolation coupling wears, causing a rhythmic rattling under load; internal bearings can also wear, producing a high-pitched whine - both failure modes are explicitly listed by UK specialist Vogue Technics as common AJ133 issues they routinely repair.

repairOptions.dealerPrice: £1,450-£2,100 (estimated based on standard UK dealer rates)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove supercharger, replace isolation coupling and snout seal, reassemble with new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses rattling noise; does not fix internal bearing wear

repairOptions.dealerPrice: £3,200-£4,600 (estimated based on standard UK dealer rates)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full supercharger strip, replace bearings, seals, coupling, and rotors if scored, bench-test boost pressure
repairOptions.longevity: Restores forced induction performance; suitable if engine block is healthy

repairOptions.dealerPrice: £9,500-£12,500 (estimated based on standard UK dealer rates)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete stripped block rebuild with upgraded supercharger coupling, new bearings/seals throughout, bench-tested, ECU-matched, 12-month warranty. Workshop-fitted.
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if supercharger failure has caused debris ingestion or if engine has other high-mileage wear

problems.recommendation: A rhythmic rattling under acceleration that disappears at idle typically indicates coupling wear - address this early to avoid debris entering the intake. If a high-pitched whine is present or boost pressure is low, the supercharger likely needs a full overhaul. For Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged models over 80,000 miles, combining supercharger repair with a full engine rebuild delivers the most reliable long-term outcome.
problems.cta: -> Compare rebuilt AJ133 engine prices for your Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged

items.question: What are the warning signs of timing chain failure on a Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged?
items.answer: Listen for a brief metallic rattle on cold start that disappears after 2-3 seconds; this indicates tensioner wear before catastrophic failure occurs on your Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged.
keyPoints: Cold-start rattling lasting under 3 seconds suggests early tensioner wear
Cam/crank synchronisation fault codes appear once guides are damaged
Metallic rattling that persists after warm-up means stop driving immediately
Oil analysis can detect aluminium particles from worn chain guides
items.warning: Continuing to drive with persistent chain rattling risks piston/valve damage requiring full engine replacement.
items.cta: -> Get a diagnostic quote from a Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged specialist today

items.question: Should I choose used, reconditioned, or rebuilt for my Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged engine replacement?
items.answer: Rebuilt AJ133 engines offer the best balance of reliability and value for Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged owners, with upgraded components addressing known failure points.
keyPoints: Used engines carry unknown mileage and hidden wear risks
Reconditioned units replace visible wear parts but may miss internal issues
Rebuilt engines include full strip, upgraded components, and bench testing
All EngineMarket suppliers provide minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.warning: Avoid used engines without compression test reports - hidden bearing wear can cause repeat failure within months.
items.cta: -> Compare rebuilt, reconditioned and used AJ133 prices for your Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged

items.question: Can I drive my Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged if the engine is knocking?
items.answer: No - stop driving immediately if your Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged engine produces metallic knocking to prevent catastrophic crankshaft damage.
keyPoints: Metallic knocking indicates bearing failure or piston contact
Continuing to drive circulates metal debris through oil galleries
Arrange recovery to a specialist workshop, do not attempt to drive
Early diagnosis can sometimes save the block for rebuild
items.warning: Driving with knocking can turn a £6,500 rebuild into a £12,000+ replacement due to irreparable block damage.
items.cta: -> Arrange emergency recovery for your Range Rover L405 LWB 5.0 Supercharged today
